title: Programming Languages
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a high-level programming language?
questions.explanation: C is a high-level language that is closer to human-readable syntax than machine code.

questions.prompt: A language that supports inheritance is usually:
questions.explanation: Inheritance is a core feature of object-oriented programming.

questions.prompt: Which parameter passing mechanism copies the value of the argument?
questions.explanation: Call by value passes a copy of the argument to the function.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a functional programming language?
questions.explanation: Haskell is a purely functional programming language.

questions.prompt: Garbage collection is mainly used to:
questions.explanation: Garbage collection reclaims memory that is no longer in use.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a dynamically typed language?
questions.explanation: Python determines variable types at runtime instead of compile time.
Polymorphism allows the same interface to be used for different underlying types.

questions.prompt: In recursion, the base case is used to:
questions.explanation: A base case terminates the recursive calls.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a feature of object-oriented programming?
questions.explanation: Pointer arithmetic is a low-level feature rather than an OOP concept.
